title: Sablé breton
subtitle: Thick, buttery Breton shortbread rounds
excerpt: The signature biscuit of Brittany: a thick round shortbread made with salted butter and egg yolks, with a golden, crumbly crumb and a grid pattern pressed into the top. Richer and thicker than a thin sablé.
sourceNotes: Sablé breton is the thick, enriched shortbread of Brittany, distinct from the thinner sablé of Normandy and the rest of France. The defining characteristics are the use of salted Breton butter (Brittany's most famous product), egg yolks for richness and colour, and the unusual baking method in a ring mould or thick pressed disc. The recipe appears in Clotilde Dusoulier's Chocolate and Zucchini (2007) and in the pastry curriculum documented by Christophe Felder in Patisserie (English edition, 2011).

content.text: Use butter with a pronounced flavour: either salted Breton butter if you can find it, or unsalted butter with the full measure of sea salt added separately. The salt is structural to the character of this biscuit, not seasoning. A sablé breton made with unsalted butter and no salt added is a different thing.
The biscuits should be deep golden, the colour of proper shortbread, not pale. A pale sablé breton is undercooked. The deep bake develops the nutty, caramelised flavour that defines the biscuit.
Make the dough and chill, 1 hour

Beat the {{unsalted-butter}} and {{caster-sugar}} until pale and fluffy. Beat in the egg yolks and {{vanilla-extract}}. Fold in the {{plain-flour}}, {{baking-powder}}, and {{fine-sea-salt}} until a smooth, slightly sticky dough forms. Wrap in cling film and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.

Heat the oven to 160°C fan. Line a baking tray with baking paper. On a lightly floured surface, roll the chilled dough to a thickness of 1 cm (much thicker than most biscuits). Cut into rounds with a 6 cm cutter and place on the tray. Brush the tops with the egg yolk glaze. Use a fork to press a crosshatch pattern into each biscuit. Bake for 22 to 25 minutes until deep golden brown all over. Cool on the tray for 10 minutes before transferring to a rack.
